seoTitle: How to Insert a LinkedIn Logo in Google Docs (2026)
metaDescription: Add a clean, hyperlinked LinkedIn icon to your resume or letterhead in Google Docs using the built-in image search and link tools.
intro: A professional resume or business card often needs a small, hyperlinked LinkedIn icon rather than a clunky pasted image. This guide shows how to find a transparent logo through Google Docs' built-in web search, resize it, and link it to your profile.

steps.heading: Open the image search panel
steps.body: Click Insert, hover over Image, and select 'Search the web' to open the sidebar search tool.

steps.heading: Search for a transparent logo
steps.body: Type 'LinkedIn logo PNG transparent' into the search field so the icon blends cleanly into your page background.

steps.heading: Insert and resize the icon
steps.body: Select a high-resolution result, click Insert, then drag the corner handles to shrink it to an appropriate size.

steps.heading: Set text wrapping
steps.body: Click the floating layout icon on the image and choose 'Wrap text' so the logo sits neatly alongside nearby text.

steps.heading: Add your profile link
steps.body: With the image selected, press Ctrl+K (or Cmd+K), paste your LinkedIn profile URL, and click Apply to make the icon clickable.

faq.question: Where can I find a properly licensed LinkedIn logo?
faq.answer: Use LinkedIn's official brand assets or a transparent PNG from a reputable source to avoid using an unauthorized version.

faq.question: Can I resize the logo without distorting it?
faq.answer: Drag from a corner handle rather than an edge handle to keep the image's proportions intact.

faq.question: Does the hyperlink work when the document is exported to PDF?
faq.answer: Yes, hyperlinks on images generally remain clickable in exported PDF files.

faq.question: Can I add other social icons the same way?
faq.answer: Yes, the same web search and link process works for any other platform's logo.
